reasoning: First iteration with significant optimization potential. GenDB is 3-5x slower than DuckDB across all queries, with clear bottlenecks identified. Multiple low-risk, high-impact optimizations available. Selecting 4 complementary optimizations: SIMD filtering (Q6), dictionary encoding (Q1, Q3), and load time optimization. These changes are well-understood, have clear implementation paths, and address the root causes. With 2 iterations remaining, we have budget for recovery if needed.
focus_areas: Q1 dictionary encoding for returnflag/linestatus (enables direct array indexing)
Q3 dictionary encoding for c_mktsegment (eliminates strcmp overhead)
Data loading optimization with pre-allocation and parallel parsing (18s → 3.6-6s target)
strategy_notes: Start with dictionary encoding changes in storage layer first, as these enable query-level optimizations. Then implement SIMD filtering for Q6 (isolated, low-risk). Finally tackle data loading optimization. All selected optimizations are low-to-medium risk with high expected payoff. This conservative first iteration establishes a foundation for more aggressive optimizations in iterations 2-3 if needed.
notes: Implementation order suggestion: (1) Add dictionary encoding to storage/storage.cpp during load (recommendations #1, #2, #4 storage changes), (2) Update Q1 aggregation to use direct array indexing instead of hash table, (3) Update Q3 filter to use integer comparison, (4) Implement SIMD filtering for Q6, (5) Optimize load functions with pre-allocation and parallel parsing. Keep changes modular so regressions are easy to diagnose. Target: Close the gap to within 2x of DuckDB on all queries.
